steps.title: Recognize the initial value
steps.content: In an exponential model $a(b)^t$, the constant $a$ is the starting value, because it is the value of the expression when $t=0$. In this model, that constant is $8$.

steps.title: Check the launch time
steps.content:
Since $t$ is years since launch, the launch time is $t=0$. Substitute $0$ for $t$:
$$S(0)=8(1.6)^0=8\cdot 1=8$$So $8$ represents the estimated number of subscribers when the newsletter launched. It is not the yearly increase or the percent increase. The answer is A.
